What is up friends? Welcome to coaching the Conversion Enrollment Framework. I am so stoked, honored, appreciative of you being here. If you're in this course, it's really going to be about shifting our sales paradigm, how we're showing up on enrollment calls, how we're leading those conversations. And really this is a major shift from disempowering conversations that force compliance, which we're going to talk about all through the course into empowering conversations, leadership-based conversations that actually encourage transformative decisions, right, in a very ethical way. So that's what this is about. We could go all into the backstory of why this is necessary, and we're going to touch upon that throughout this course. But for now, I just want to welcome you here and appreciate you taking that stand for yourself, for your business, and for all those who will have the privilege of having these conversations with you in your business. So, many reasons this program exists, right? The first major reason is to answer this question. Does sales have to be hard, right? That's the kind of question. Is it even safe to call it sales, or does that word itself bring up this self-contraction, where we feel all sorts of triggering emotions around it, thoughts and stories of what we even believe sales to be, right, and what that demands of us on these calls, where we never quite really feel ourselves, where we kind of feel like we're playing into a script that isn't of our own, and we don't feel authentic and true to who we actually are, and what our values are, and what our business represents. So that's what this course is here to answer, is does sales have to be so hard? And what can it be, right, when we really redefine at its core what it is and how we can show up differently to do it? So two major goals in this program. The first one is going to be to turn sales, enrollment, conversation strategy calls, whatever you like to call them in your own world, into the most expansive, enjoyable, and profitable task in your business, both for yourself and for your pre-client. And the reason is because it should be, right? This is literally the most transformative ecosystem that you're going to have with your pre-client before they join your program, right? This should feel incredible for yourself, for that person on the call with you. Nothing should feel better. It shouldn't feel better to send out a PDF and have that read. It shouldn't feel better for someone to consume an evergreen webinar. This should be the moment where you get to be in the fullest expression of what you, your business, your program represents, and the person on the other end of that gets to receive the full download of what's possible and available to them should they continue on that journey with you. The next is to clear up all the confusions, distortions, and twisted paradigms that have perhaps made it less than a stellar experience in the past. And you've probably been on both sides of these twisted, distorted paradigms. And we're going to talk about that for a little bit. So the current sales paradigm, the one that I would say still kind of reigns supreme. If you got on a strategy called chances are you would experience some degree of these elements of the paradigm, right? They tend to be a little bit more high pressure, right? Forcing decisions prematurely. Weaponized psychology, right? To take a look at weaponized psychology, look at all the persuasion signs, all the advertising books in existence right now. It kind of takes these mind hacks, these biases, all these elements of psychology that people have discovered, right? And finds out ways to weaponize it against the person on the other end. So I'm just calling it what it is. It's not empowering psychology. It's taking biases of the human mind and the emotional mind and actually weaponizing it and gains the user, which I don't think is really cool. One aspect of that is gaslighting, where anything you say on a call cannon will be held against you at the close. You hear about this all the time. The classic is in a moment of less resistance at the beginning. Someone will ask you how important is this goal to you, right? And you'll kind of say 10 on 10, most important thing in the world, right? And then when the context totally changes, right? When it's in the context of a program at $10,000 price or whatever it is, they hold that against you, right? I thought you were committed, right? That is a form of sales driven gaslighting. So we'll talk all about that. And essentially the mood, the vibe, the energy of these calls is it kind of treats the prospect as a conquest, right? Through the systematic dismantling of their faculties, disempowering them by dismantling their self-confidence, self-trust and ability to make decisions that actually work for them versus work for you or work for their company or work for whoever they're talking to on the other end of it. And a positive result, a sale essentially isn't empowered. It's really just forced compliance when this exhaustion sets in, when this disorientation is so massive that they just end up kind of complying with what the salesperson is proposing to get themselves off the freaking line. So that's kind of what we see a lot of in the current sales paradigm. One part about that that you may have also seen is what I call kind of like the spiritually clothed conquest. Now a lot of people will still operate under this current sales paradigm but twist things even further, right? To make themselves feel better about what they're doing. So this is where you'll often get all of those things just trojan-horst in more spiritual language such as if you're not doing everything you possibly can to close your prospect, you're doing them into service, right? How many of us have heard that before? And I'm asking that rhetorically since you're not actually here with me, but you are here with me, so let me know. Like, is this something we've heard before? Written or on the line with someone? And the thing about this and statements like this is it's disguised leadership, right? When someone feels they're actually leading you and being of service when they encourage someone to max out their credit cards and sell their grandmother's pass down ring on Craigslist in order to join the program. Who is that actually of service to? So we have to be very careful in our discernment, right? Because a lot of this shitty sales paradigm ends up being spiritually clothed trojan-horst where the person running this paradigm has convinced themselves, right? That they're actually of service and it's important to call that out especially if we're going to try to dismantle that paradigm and step into something that truly is of service. So this is an actively distorted paradigm that still has its grip on the industry and as a result has created a bit of a weak limp-wristed counter-response creating two camps. So Camp A, the one we just spoke about, Hardcore, Borderline, Sociopathic, if we're going to be honest about it and it's really about getting the sale at all costs, always be closing no matter what and they're really being powerful. Their power is generated from a place of insecurity and we know this because they need to disempower the other person in order to feel powerful themselves and they rely solely on this spiritually cloaked gaslighting and fear-mongering convincing themselves that they're being of service as they slowly word by word chip away at their prospects, free will, capacities, desires and sovereignty and ability to actually be empowered in their own right and the end goal is the sale but the sale as a result of forced compliance. Then we have the pendulum swinging a little too far to the other side where we have Camp B which is loose, flimsy and just kind of like overly sensitive, right? They are deathly afraid of coming off as Camp A and as such they have very little structure or leadership they don't want to be threatening through their leadership or over assertive they feel insecure in asking big questions or confidently asserting or suggesting anything for that matter they're not making powerful invitations, they're not asking powerful questions and they kind of just put it fully in the prospects hand to direct themselves through the process and this is also coming from a place of insecurity being overly concerned around how they're perceived and ultimately valuing and wanting to be light and safe rather than actually trusting that they are ethical and safe and acknowledging that they can't control the perception of the other person all they can do is trust who they are and show up authentically to that So overall this person in Camp B feels too much empathy for even a little bit of discomfort that arises on the call failing to recognize and acknowledge that change, evolution and transformation all these things you're getting on the call often and naturally do involve uncomfortable feelings of resistance and friction along the way so this is something we actually have to become comfortable with, develop a stronger wider threshold for what we allow ourselves to experience on a call with a pre-customer or a pre-client So Camp C is what we're going to be exploring today, I call it empathetic leadership this coach, this enrollment specialist recognizes that empowerment isn't a zero sum game you don't need to take it away from the person you're on the line with in order to feel empowered yourself you actually grow together and the best client relationships are co-led where you will lead parts of the call and you'll invite them to lead certain parts of the call they're co-empowered and they're ultimately co-created so we're going to talk about all of these elements as we go along and here's the point, compassion and strength aren't mutually exclusive but serve one another you can feel compassion and empathy towards your prospect without buying in fully into their story and you can still be strong and invite them to explore other options you can invite them to explore new possibilities that they may not have felt courageous enough to explore on their own or maybe they didn't have that insight without your presence there so these two things aren't mutually exclusive but actually in service of one another and the end goal of Camp C is to have an empowered decision not force compliance but a decision where someone actually feels like they decided something that is in the highest service to them their family, all the other stakeholders who are involved in that decision and who just feels fucking awesome getting off the line and beginning a new journey with you so that is the goal of Camp C and you gotta know you can be compassionate and empathetic without orienting around their discomfort but rather giving them a chance to orient around your leadership and confidence in yourself, in your program and ultimately in their potential to achieve the result and overcome the problem that they're coming to you for I call this the art of state sharing so share your power, your confidence and your certainty these are up to you to share and invite someone to experience with you instead of you taking on their fear, closure, resistance so instead of that you generously offer to share your state, you light their torch rather than you each stumbling through the dark so that's gonna be the whole goal of the way we run the CTC Enrollment Framework that's our focus, here's how we're gonna get you there in Module 1 I'm going to steep you in coaching the conversion as it pertains to the enrollment call environment so you may be familiar with this through our other CTC programs you know Ten X Sanchez, Ten X Sales Pages, Minimum Vibe Launch 2.0, wherever you came from or whatever you've already seen, we're going to adapt this framework to what it looks like on a sales call which is the most dynamic environment possible and exciting one so we're gonna talk about ethics and ethos dispelling more of that old paradigm shit that may have just kind of still seeped in, pre-call mindsets, how to really get yourself in the space where you can show up and be the torch lighter, the torch bearer instead of being kind of consumed by their energy and their story on the call we're gonna talk about enrollment as an emergent, how an empowered decision isn't forced it's not this major domino that you have to pound against until it finally cracks down it's actually something that naturally falls down if you essentially coach the right milestones throughout your sales call we're gonna talk about scripts versus frameworks, a lot of people are running scripts on their sales calls which feel very rigid and linear and don't allow them to actually have that dynamic aspect that a sales call demands that's actually natural to that environment and then finally we're gonna teach you the CTC enrollment framework that's going to give you the overview of how this course is gonna go down Module 2, we're gonna talk about the meta-states for an empowered enrollment about connecting to safety, connecting to power, connecting to possibility this is really the 80-20, if you can have someone connected to their safety, to their power, and to what's possible everything else kind of becomes an emergent so we're gonna talk about how to coach and facilitate those states Module 3 is really where the juice is, we're gonna talk about unpacking situational gestalts that's a big crazy word, don't worry about that right now we're gonna break down exactly what that is we're gonna talk about all sorts of coaching techniques in order to unpack the gestalt that represents the problem or the outcome that they're trying to achieve as well as some challenges through it, right? you may come up against defensiveness around people distancing themselves not wanting to really feel the extent of what they're trying to do and what they're trying to overcome and even deflecting the situation, a lot of us have kind of had calls where people will start asking us about our lives, right? about our relationships, about how we did X, Y, or Z and that's cool, right? that's a natural defense, but it's actually them deflecting the situation onto you because they're a little bit uncomfortable diving into their own personal experience then we're gonna talk about permissions, right? permission at the beginning of the call around agenda-sending, around openings to create a safe container there as well as permission at the end before we transition into the offer then module 4 is gonna be about helping them actually actualize all this potential that we created and established in part 1 so we're gonna be coaching, achievability, actualization, and accountability we're gonna walk through how to share what I call the integrated offer that takes everything in part 1 into account presenting investment options in a confident natural way where this isn't this pressurized situation where, wow, I finally got to drop the price and everyone goes silent and it's all this weird, awkward silence scaring us and overall shittiness, we're gonna talk about natural ways to do that as well as the challenges and concerns that come up in this juncture how to actually approach objections without calling them or considering them objections, right? because they're not necessarily objecting to anything they are using that as a way to arrow concerns about themselves, the opportunity, you, whatever it is and these are all mostly valid and must be treated as valid next we'll talk about ethical urgency and what I call the 100% close the standard we hold for ourselves and our customers that ultimately everyone makes a decision, right? whether that's a yes, whether that's a no, that's a boundary that I believe it's up to us to set if we are going to be in leadership, right? no one wins by sitting on the fence and as long as we make it equally safe to say yes and no that is a standard that I believe we get to ethically hold ourselves and our customers too and then next steps really about how to take all this, put it all together some other trainings that we have within the CTC university, automated intimacy universe that may plug in well and help serve you along your journey as you begin to integrate this so that's all we got who this is for I'm sure you're in the right spot if you made it this far but this is for coaches, consultants, experts, course creators, therapists, service providers anyone who is offering what I call a life changing program who feels they need a more empowering leadership based way of enrolling clients where you're actually being of service, not just using that as a buzzword who it's not for this probably won't be fully applicable, it doesn't mean you won't be able to take parts of it and implement it and recontextualize it for what you're doing but this wasn't designed and optimized for software demos so anyone who's selling SaaS, especially enterprise enterprise business to business products with multiple stakeholders and decision makers this probably isn't going to be for you luxury items, high-end e-commerce, probably not for you either although there's always going to be elements that you can take and recontextualize it to your situation or to your client situation if you're helping run sales and enrollment for a client and likewise probably not going to be completely applicable for anyone who's doing custom done for you services with extremely wide ranging scopes this works more for program based programs program based programs that's a little redundant anyway why are we doing this anyway? this whole framework it's humanistic and empathetic so we want to work with humans who are vastly implicated in the decision or indecision we're not working with, I don't know, emotionless spocks for lack of a better word who aren't implicated in the decision they're trying to make so the starting point that I'd love for you to have if you're taking this program is that you have a validated offer that you feel super confident in because you've already successfully enrolled and served people with it so this confidence that I'm asking you to have is key for empathetic conversation conversion coaches otherwise you'll likely self-sabotage to protect their interests so I remember back in the early days when I was selling programs my own programs even that didn't even have a single student didn't even have a single case study yet and I was really getting on these first initial calls I didn't have enough confidence in my own program or in myself to fulfill and that's why I was so flimsy on these sales costs because I was almost like please don't buy it, please don't buy it I don't want to be responsible if this is a big fuck up and you don't get your results, right? so that confidence in knowing that what you have is solid what you have works and you've actually walked people through it before is really the 80-20 in my view of showing up with confidence especially when you could re-tap in to those results before every call so our big promise for this program is you will achieve the highest close rates you've ever had that you feel incredible about while building longer lasting and more successful client relationships because they began from a place of respect, trust and empowerment not forced compliance, the fear mongering and gaslighting and all the other shitty things we're going to talk about later so whether or not this immediately results in higher close rates or not depends how much of this forced compliance are you working with right now it's totally possible that if you're doing everything and pulling all the tricks that you no longer can will yourself to do then the short term, your close rates might take a little bit of a dip as you take tools off the table but as you reintegrate these new tools and these new paradigms and you get well-practiced in it two things will happen, one you'll feel incredible about these calls you're not going to self-sabotage it every time you're on a call you're not going to try to protect your prospect from not buying by not doing your job essentially and you're just going to show up in a different way, right? and the end goal of this is for those people who do join to be so prepared, so lit up, so ready to actually get a result in your program that the lifetime value there more than makes up for any temporary dip and close rates but our goal as I said is to get you those highest, your highest close rates yet right, while stripping away the things that no longer feel good and adding new coaching techniques, new skills, new mindsets, new abilities that will help get you there so that's our promise, that's what we're up to I'm once again so honored that you've decided to take this journey with us and I'll see you in that next lesson, bye for now
